topic: short red-wine maceration and reduced colour extraction
trigger_keywords: cuál de los siguientes factores puede reducir la intensidad de color en un vino tinto

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Red must spends only a short period in contact with grape skins.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: The limited contact time gives less opportunity for anthocyanins and other colour compounds to diffuse from the skins into the wine.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: All else being equal, the resulting red wine has lower colour intensity than one given a longer effective maceration.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Short maceration limits skin-contact time and therefore colour extraction.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
